methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Finwave Semiconductor is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers in Chips & Semiconductors (including Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, and Rain AI at the same stage) and 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), though public comparables cannot be directly applied without disclosed revenue.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors to the $25M raised as a proxy for implied current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $50M. Peer-median valuation across four same-sector, same-stage companies produces a $1.5B median, with a range from $500M to $5.0B. The public-comp anchoring approach (public semiconductors trade at 20.6x P/S median) is inapplicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $42M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $775M. The substantial width reflects tension between the stage-progression method's conservative $50M result and peer-median's substantially higher $1.5B anchor. Key uncertainties include product readiness, addressable market definition relative to peers, and whether peer valuations reflect sustainable demand or near-term correction risk. Further diligence on revenue trajectory and customer traction would narrow this range materially.
